_note: This file is a design-time snapshot and may be out of date. For current step state, run: iw item-status <ID> --json. The DB is the authoritative source of truth (CR-00023).
title: Performance-budget test layer — pytest-benchmark assertions with regression-alert baselines

steps.description: Add `pytest-benchmark>=4.0,<5` to `pyproject.toml` `[dependency-groups] dev` and regenerate `uv.lock`. Register a `perf` marker in `pyproject.toml` `[tool.pytest.ini_options].markers` AND append `and not perf` to existing `addopts` so default test runs do NOT collect perf modules. Create `tests/perf/__init__.py`, `tests/perf/conftest.py` (mark all tests in this package with `@pytest.mark.perf` automatically; provide a `seeded_orch_db` testcontainer fixture that runs `Base.metadata.create_all()` + `FTS_FUNCTION_SQL` + `FTS_TRIGGER_SQL` + seeds 1 project / 3 work items / 1 batch / 1 active worktree-stub row), and `tests/perf/test_daemon_poll_loop.py`. The daemon perf test must: (1) import `Daemon._poll_cycle` from `orch/daemon/main.py`, (2) build a minimal `Daemon` instance against the testcontainer DB (mock external integrations: keep-alive poller, doc-job poller — invoke ONLY the `_poll_cycle` method), (3) run pytest-benchmark with `warmup_rounds=5, rounds=10`, (4) assert `mean < BUDGET_MS` (or `min` if σ/μ > 0.3 — record the ratio in module docstring), (5) BUDGET_MS is a module-level constant set to `initial_mean × 1.5` based on S01's measurement run. RED evidence: record the initial mean and final assertion in the report's `tdd_red_evidence` field as 'daemon poll-loop initial mean = X ms; BUDGET_MS = ceil(X * 1.5) = Y ms; final test passes with mean = Z < Y'. Save baseline JSON to `tests/perf/baselines/daemon.json` via `pytest-benchmark --benchmark-save=daemon --benchmark-storage=file://tests/perf/baselines`. Add Makefile target `test-perf-daemon` (in `.PHONY`) that runs `uv run pytest tests/perf/test_daemon_poll_loop.py -v --benchmark-compare=daemon --benchmark-compare-fail=mean:25% --benchmark-storage=file://tests/perf/baselines -m perf --no-cov`. NO production code changes. NO migrations. Run `make lint` + `make format` + `make typecheck` pre-flight on touched files; targeted `uv run pytest tests/perf/test_daemon_poll_loop.py -v` for self-verification (do NOT run `make test-unit` or `make test-integration`).

Add `tests/perf/test_rag_query.py` and `tests/perf/test_dashboard_routes.py`. RAG perf test: build a small deterministic in-memory LanceDB index fixture (10 documents), STUB the embedding model with a deterministic hash-to-fixed-dim function (NO Ollama dependency — perf test must run unconditionally, opposite stance to `tests/integration/rag/`'s skip-when-no-Ollama hook), invoke `CodeQA.answer_stream` (drain the async generator into a list), assert `mean < BUDGET_S` per the same module-docstring methodology as S01 (mean vs min based on σ/μ > 0.3), pytest-benchmark `warmup_rounds=5, rounds=10`, save baseline JSON to `tests/perf/baselines/rag.json`. Dashboard perf test: parametrize over exactly these 5 routes — `/`, `/project/{id}/queue`, `/project/{id}/batches`, `/project/{id}/jobs`, `/project/{id}/code` — using FastAPI `TestClient` against the `seeded_orch_db` fixture from S01's conftest, perform ≥3 warmup hits per route (excluded from measurement), then measure p50 over ≥10 runs per route, assert each route's p50 < per-route BUDGET_MS_<ROUTE> constant set to `initial_p50 × 1.5`, save baseline JSON to `tests/perf/baselines/routes.json`. Add Makefile targets (all in `.PHONY`): `test-perf-rag` (per-module), `test-perf-routes` (per-module), `test-perf` (umbrella — chains daemon → rag → routes), `test-perf-update-baseline` (operator-only — re-runs all three modules with `--benchmark-save=<module>` to regenerate baselines; the recipe MUST print a one-line warning 'Baseline updated locally — commit requires CR review per CR-00083 Notes section' to stdout). RED evidence: record initial means and budgets for both modules. NO production code changes. NO migrations.

Create `.github/workflows/perf-budgets.yml` with: (1) triggers — `schedule: - cron: '17 3 * * *'` (nightly 03:17 UTC, offset to avoid collision with other workflows) AND `workflow_dispatch:` (manual); NO `pull_request` trigger; (2) one job `perf-budgets` running on `ubuntu-latest` consistently (no matrix — runner variance must stay constant); (3) standard setup steps (checkout, install uv, `uv sync --all-extras --dev`); (4) `make test-perf` as the main step; (5) `actions/upload-artifact@v4` step uploading `.benchmarks/**` JSON output on success or failure (`if: always()`); (6) a final failure step (`if: failure()`) that appends a one-line entry under a 'Perf regression follow-ups' subsection in `ai-dev/work/TESTS_ENHANCEMENT.md` referencing the workflow run URL, then commits + pushes via a `peter-evans/create-pull-request` (or equivalent — match the pattern in existing workflows like `e2e.yml`). Update `docs/IW_AI_Core_Testing_Strategy.md`: §2 add new 'Layer 8 — Performance budgets (`tests/perf/` — CR-00083)' subsection covering daemon poll-loop, RAG query, dashboard routes, budget methodology (initial measurement + 50% headroom), mean-vs-min rule, baseline-update policy (operator-only + CR review); §5 add new row 'perf-budgets nightly: mean<budget, regression > 25% fails' linked to the new workflow; §9 flip row '4.2 Performance budgets' status → ✅ with CR-00083; §11 add new dated changelog entry (2026-05-24) summarising the Phase 4 first ship. Update `skills/iw-ai-core-testing/SKILL.md` §4 with a new 'Performance budgets (CR-00083, Phase 4 item 4.2)' section covering: when to add a perf test (any new hot path), how to choose a budget (initial measurement + 50% headroom — methodology mirrors the strategy doc), the no-tautology rule applied to perf assertions (assert against a specific budget constant, NOT against pytest-benchmark's auto-comparison alone — the assertion must be a real upper bound), and the operator-only baseline-bump policy. Run `iw sync-skills --force iw-ai-core-testing` to mirror to `.claude/skills/iw-ai-core-testing/SKILL.md` (verify byte-identical via `diff skills/iw-ai-core-testing/SKILL.md .claude/skills/iw-ai-core-testing/SKILL.md`). Update `ai-dev/work/TESTS_ENHANCEMENT.md`: §8 row 4.2 status → 'DONE — CR-00083 (2026-05-24)' with a one-line summary; v1.3 header status block → v1.4 header noting Phase 4 first item shipped (CR-00083 — perf budgets); §11 add a new dated changelog entry (2026-05-24) describing what shipped (3 perf modules, 5 Makefile targets, 1 nightly workflow, threshold start = 25% mean regression). NO production code changes outside the workflow YAML + docs + skill + tracker.

Review S01 + S02 together. Verify: (1) `pytest-benchmark>=4.0,<5` present in `pyproject.toml` `[dependency-groups] dev` and `uv.lock` is regenerated cleanly. (2) `perf` marker registered in `[tool.pytest.ini_options].markers` AND `addopts` excludes `perf` via `and not perf` so default `make test-unit` / `make test-integration` do NOT collect perf modules — confirm by inspecting `pyproject.toml`. (3) `tests/perf/conftest.py` auto-applies `@pytest.mark.perf` to all tests in the package (via `pytest_collection_modifyitems` or `pytestmark`); seeded testcontainer fixture runs `Base.metadata.create_all()` + `FTS_FUNCTION_SQL` + `FTS_TRIGGER_SQL` AND uses `postgresql+psycopg` (NOT `psycopg2`) URL replacement. (4) Each perf test asserts against a module-level BUDGET constant (NOT a tautology like `assert mean > 0` or `assert min < float('inf')`). (5) Each test docstring records whether it uses `mean` or `min` and the σ/μ ratio rationale if `min`. (6) Baselines exist at `tests/perf/baselines/daemon.json`, `tests/perf/baselines/rag.json`, `tests/perf/baselines/routes.json` and are non-empty. (7) Makefile targets `test-perf-daemon`, `test-perf-rag`, `test-perf-routes`, `test-perf`, `test-perf-update-baseline` all exist AND appear in `.PHONY` AND `test-perf` chains the three modules. (8) RAG perf test does NOT depend on a running Ollama listener (stub is in place); dashboard perf test performs ≥3 warmup hits per route. (9) NO production code (`orch/**`, `dashboard/**`, `executor/**`) modified — diff against main is bounded to `tests/perf/**`, `pyproject.toml`, `uv.lock`, `Makefile`. (10) Pre-review gate: `make lint` + `make format-check` show zero new violations on touched files. (11) TDD RED evidence in both S01 and S02 reports records the initial-measurement → budget-set → final-green narrative (not 'n/a').

Global cross-agent review covering AC1–AC8 holistically. AC1: `pytest-benchmark>=4.0,<5` installed (re-verify). AC2/AC3/AC4: spot-check each perf test for a real BUDGET constant + warmup/rounds + mean-vs-min rationale + ≥10 measurement rounds; for one of the three modules, also stash-revert a budget tightening (cut budget in half), re-run the targeted test, confirm it goes red, restore — only if safe in this worktree. AC5: enumerate every Makefile target, confirm 5 perf targets exist, all in `.PHONY`, `test-perf` is a true umbrella, `test-perf-update-baseline` prints the operator warning. AC6: inject a synthetic 30% regression via `time.sleep(<delta>)` in one perf test, run `make test-<module>`, confirm it fails with a diff message naming the regression percentage; revert the sleep, re-run, confirm green. AC7: open the workflow YAML; verify nightly cron + `workflow_dispatch` triggers, NO `pull_request` trigger, `make test-perf` as the main step, artifact upload on `if: always()`, follow-up tracker append on `if: failure()`. AC8: verify all four documentation surfaces consistent — strategy doc §2 Layer 8 + §5 row + §9 row 4.2 flip + §11 changelog all dated 2026-05-24 with CR-00083; skill §4 new section synced byte-identical to `.claude/skills/`; tracker §8 row 4.2 DONE + v1.4 header + §11 changelog all dated 2026-05-24 with CR-00083; cross-reference IDs and dates match across surfaces. Diff against main touches ONLY `tests/perf/**`, `pyproject.toml`, `uv.lock`, `Makefile`, `.github/workflows/perf-budgets.yml`, `docs/IW_AI_Core_Testing_Strategy.md`, `skills/iw-ai-core-testing/**`, `.claude/skills/iw-ai-core-testing/**`, `ai-dev/work/TESTS_ENHANCEMENT.md` — no `orch/`, `dashboard/`, `executor/`, `scripts/`, `bin/`, `templates/`, no other workflows, no other skills. No scope creep (no new markers beyond `perf`, no new gates added to `make check`, no new CI workflows beyond `perf-budgets.yml`, no production-code change). Run `make lint` + `make format-check` + `make type-check` + `make test-unit` + targeted `uv run pytest tests/perf/ -m perf -v --no-cov` for self-verification (do NOT run `make check` — that's S10/S11/S12's job).
QV: Linting (ruff check + JS check + Jinja2 template check)

QV: Assertion scanner — perf-module assertions must be real budget comparisons, NOT tautologies. The scanner runs against `tests/` (including the new `tests/perf/`); a perf test that only asserts `assert mean >= 0` would be caught here.
QV: Unit tests (perf modules MUST be excluded via the `not perf` marker filter — failing here would mean the addopts wiring is wrong)
QV: Integration tests (perf modules MUST be excluded — `tests/perf/` is not under `tests/integration/` so the path filter excludes them; failing here would mean accidental cross-collection)

QV: Diff coverage (the perf modules themselves are new test files, not production code, so they contribute to test surface — not to covered lines; this gate confirms no production code was accidentally added)
QV: Secret scanning (gitleaks) — no secrets expected in the workflow YAML or perf modules; this gate confirms

Self-assessment of the just-completed item via the iw-item-analyze skill. Look for: (a) did any S01/S02 budget have to be revised mid-step because the initial measurement was an outlier (would suggest the 10-run baseline methodology needs more rounds); (b) did the RAG embedding stub raise any unexpected coupling concerns; (c) did any QV gate burn a fix cycle around perf module collection (the `not perf` marker wiring is the most likely failure mode); (d) compare against Phase 1/2/3 CR patterns — CR-00059 (mutmut spike), CR-00060 (Hypothesis property tests), CR-00061 (quarantine workflow), CR-00072 (contract sweep), CR-00075 (security tests) — for setup-cost patterns and look for the same shape; (e) did the strategy doc + skill + tracker triple update stay consistent in one round of fixes (a recurring failure mode in CR-00072/73/74/75/76 was inconsistent dates/IDs across the three surfaces).
